source: ECI — Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) and VVPAT

chunks.heading: What is an EVM and how does it work?
chunks.text:
Q: What is an Electronic Voting Machine (EVM)?

A: An Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) is an electronic device used to record votes in Indian elections. It has two units:

1. Control Unit (CU): Operated by the Presiding Officer. Controls the ballot unit and records/counts votes.
2. Ballot Unit (BU): The voter-facing unit displaying candidate names and symbols with a blue button against each.

The two units are connected by a five-metre cable. EVMs are manufactured by Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), Bengaluru, and Electronics Corporation of India Limited (ECIL), Hyderabad — both government undertakings.

Key features:
- Battery-operated — not connected to external power supply
- Completely standalone — not connected to internet, Bluetooth, or any network
- Can record a maximum of 2,000 votes
- Can accommodate up to 64 candidates
- Once the result button is pressed on the Control Unit, candidate-wise vote tally is displayed

chunks.heading: Step-by-step guide to voting using an EVM at a polling booth
chunks.text:
Q: How does a voter cast their vote using an EVM? What is the process?

A: Step-by-step voting process at a polling booth:

Step 1: Arrive at your designated polling booth. Your name must be on the electoral roll for that specific booth.
Step 2: Show your photo identity document to the polling officer — EPIC or any of the 12 alternative IDs.
Step 3: The polling officer verifies your identity and marks your name in the electoral roll. Your left index finger is marked with indelible ink.
Step 4: You are given a voter slip and directed to the voting compartment.
Step 5: Inside the voting compartment, press the blue button on the Ballot Unit against the name and symbol of your chosen candidate. A red light appears next to your choice and a beep confirms your vote.
Step 6: The VVPAT machine prints a slip showing your chosen candidate for 7 seconds through a transparent window. After 7 seconds it drops automatically into the sealed VVPAT box.
Step 7: Exit the compartment. Your voting is complete.

Note: You can vote ONLY ONCE. Once pressed, the EVM locks for that voter.

chunks.heading: What is VVPAT and why is it important?
chunks.text:
Q: What is VVPAT? Why was it introduced?

A: VVPAT stands for Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail. It is a printer device attached to the Ballot Unit of the EVM.

How it works: When a voter presses the ballot button, the VVPAT prints a paper slip showing:
- Serial number of the candidate
- Name of the candidate
- Election symbol of the candidate

This slip is visible through a transparent window for exactly 7 seconds, then automatically drops into a sealed drop box.

Why VVPAT was introduced:
- To give voters verifiable confirmation their vote was recorded correctly
- To provide an independent paper audit trail for disputed results
- To increase transparency and voter confidence

VVPAT machines are deployed at ALL polling stations across India. VVPAT slips from 5 EVMs per assembly segment are mandatorily counted and matched with EVM count (Supreme Court order) for verification.

IMPORTANT: The voter CANNOT take the VVPAT slip. It drops automatically into the sealed box.

chunks.heading: Can EVMs be tampered with or hacked?
chunks.text:
Q: Can EVMs be tampered with or hacked?

A: The ECI and EVM manufacturers have addressed this comprehensively:

1. Not networked: EVMs are never connected to internet, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, or any external network. Remote hacking is technically impossible.

2. One-time programmable chip: The microcontroller chip is programmed once at manufacturing and cannot be reprogrammed. Software is burnt into the chip permanently.

3. Physical security: EVMs are stored in strong rooms with multiple locks, CCTV, and security personnel. All candidate representatives can seal and guard strong rooms.

4. First-level checking (FLC): Before every election, EVMs are checked in the presence of political party representatives. Mock polls are conducted to verify accuracy.

5. Double randomization: EVMs are randomly assigned to polling stations through two randomization rounds so no one can predict which EVM goes where.

6. VVPAT verification: VVPAT slips serve as an independent audit mechanism.

The ECI has repeatedly offered open challenges to anyone claiming EVM tampering ability under controlled conditions. No credible tampering has been demonstrated in Indian courts.

chunks.heading: What is a Mock Poll? Why is it conducted?
chunks.text:
Q: What is a Mock Poll and why is it conducted before voting?

A: A Mock Poll is a mandatory test voting exercise conducted on EVMs before actual polling begins on election day.

Purpose: To verify that the EVM is functioning correctly and recording votes accurately before real votes are cast.

Process:
1. On election day, before the first real voter, polling agents of all candidates are invited to witness.
2. At least 50 mock votes (or 5% of total votes, whichever is more) are cast on the EVM.
3. Result is checked to ensure each button press recorded exactly one vote for the correct candidate.
4. After verification, mock poll data is cleared (reset to zero) in the presence of all polling agents.
5. A certificate is signed by all present confirming the Mock Poll result.
6. Only then does actual voting begin.

Polling agents have the right to request a Mock Poll. If any discrepancy is found, that EVM is immediately replaced.

chunks.heading: Facilities for disabled voters using EVMs
chunks.text:
Q: Are there facilities for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) to vote using EVMs?

A: Yes, the ECI has made several provisions:

1. Braille Ballot Sheet: All EVMs have a Braille ballot sheet with raised numbers for visually impaired voters.
2. Wheelchair access: All polling stations require ramp access. Wheelchair users get priority entry.
3. Companion facility: A PwD voter may bring a companion (18+ years old) to assist inside the voting compartment. The companion can only assist one voter.
4. Priority queue: PwD voters do not need to wait in the general queue.
5. Saksham App: Allows PwD voters to request home transportation, wheelchair, and home voting.
6. Home voting (Doorstep voting): PwD voters with 40%+ benchmark disability can apply for postal ballot or a mobile polling team to visit their home.

To register as a PwD voter, you need to be marked as PwD in the electoral roll via Form 8 with a disability certificate.
